description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly oval profile and a slightly pointed end. Its surface is predominantly gray with patches of lighter and darker tones, giving it a mottled appearance. The stone exhibits subtle veining and natural textural variations, with some areas appearing smoother and others more granular. There are visible signs of weathering, such as minor surface abrasions and a generally matte finish, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. A small section of the stone bears a handwritten marking, likely an identification code. The overall form is solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications.
description: A mottled gray, irregularly shaped stone with subtle veining and a weathered, matte surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, most rounded side of the stone, highlighting its mottled gray coloration and the visible identification marking.
description_view_1: The stone appears more elongated and pointed from this angle, with a pronounced ridge running along its length.
description_view_2: This side reveals a smoother, slightly convex surface with less visible veining and a more uniform gray tone.
description_view_3: The stone's pointed end and the identification marking are prominent, with the surface texture appearing rougher and more angular.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a decorative barrier
attribute_pattern: Natural mottling and subtle veining with irregular, organic distribution; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Matte and slightly abraded, with minor surface smoothing indicative of moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones; may contrast with paved surfaces or concrete curbs.
